== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山梨県の神社]] [[Category:北杜市の歴史]] [[Category:北杜市の建築物]] [[Category:山梨県の旧郷社]] [[Category:甲斐国の式内社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:pages]] == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:pages]] == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:pages]] == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:pages]] == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:pages]] == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007 * Yamanashi Prefectural Library (ed.), 'Kai Province Shrine/Temple Records Vol.1,' Yamanashi Prefectural Library, Dec 1967 * Akeno Village Board of Education (ed.), 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village,' Sept 1984 * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Akeno Village Board of Education |title=Bunkazai Akeno |publisher=Akeno Village Board of Education |date=1988-07 |ref={{SfnRef|明野教育委員会|1988}}}} * Akeno Village (ed.), '1990 Akeno Village Statistical Overview,' Dec 1990 * Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association (ed.), 'Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' Sept 1985 * 'Machi-Mu Guidebook, Hokuto Akeno area' Tsunagu NPO, Jun 2011 * Yamanashi Festivals/Rites Investigation Committee (ed.), 'Festivals and Rites of Yamanashi: Investigation Report,' Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education, Mar 1999 * 'Cultural Properties of Akeno Village' Hokuto Archaeological Center * Ozawa Itarō (ed.), 'Kyōhoku Shrine Gazetteer,' Kyōhoku Shrine Parishioners Association, Feb 1962 * New Akeno Village History Editorial Committee (ed.), 'New Akeno Village History Main Volume,' Mar 1996 === External links === [https://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6075 ・Uwato Shrine (former gōsha) (Yamanashi Shrine Office site)] {{Commonscat|Uwato-jinja (Hokuto Akeno)}} {{Pref-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5090005006731 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町上手937', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山梨県北杜市明野町大字上手北組', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (北杜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111495856}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uwato Shrine |画像 = [[File:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社.jpg|250px]]
Uwato Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}937 Kamite, Akeno-chō |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Akeno-chō, {{ill|lt=Hokuto|Q825272|WD=Q825272|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|WD=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Its deities are {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=Akeno Village History, main volume |edition=new edition |date=March 1996 |publisher=Akeno Village |pages=859,1267}}. Festival days are April 10 and October 5. The April grand annual festival date has been changed to the 2nd Sunday of April{{Cite book|和書 |title=Festivals and rites of Yamanashi - investigation report - |date=March 1999 |publisher=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education |page=125}}. === History === The founding era is unknown. However, a stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears inscription "{{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041|1=864}})," and the shrine is treated as an Engishiki {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, so it is inferred to have existed at least by the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} period ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})[https://www.yamanashi-kankou.jp/rekitabi/jisha/spot/130.html Uwato Shrine] Yamanashi Tourism Organization "Travel Yamanashi history"Yamanashi Prefecture High School History/Civics Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walk in Yamanashi' (Historical Walk 19), Yamakawa Shuppansha, 2007, pp.186-187. It is also known that {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} prayed here for victory during his Saku campaign ({{ill|lt=Battle of Odaibara|Q1056249|WD=Q1056249|1=Battle of Odaihara}}). The current haiden was rebuilt in April 1980, and the kaguraden was newly built{{Cite book|和書 |title=Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |date=Sept 1985 |publisher=Yamanashi Shinto Youth Association |page=287}}. This shrine has a stone torii. It is Myōjin-style and the only one of that form in former Akeno Village. Its height is 2.52 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}}, but pillar diameter is as much as 43 {{ill|lt=centimeters|Q174728|WD=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, giving a stout appearance. Built in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it is considered one of the oldest torii in Japan. Similar torii exists at {{ill|lt=Katte Shrine (Nirasaki)|Q116979329|WD=Q116979329|lt=Katte Shrine|1=Katte Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nirasaki|Q290326|WD=Q290326|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, same prefecture. === Precinct === Within the precinct are the honden, haiden, kaguraden, watariden, and shamusho. [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の拝殿.jpg|代替文=Haiden of Uwato Shrine|サムネイル|Haiden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社 の神楽殿.jpg|サムネイル|Kaguraden]] [[ファイル:北杜市明野町 宇波刀神社の拝殿と本殿.jpg|サムネイル|Haiden, watariden, honden]] === Cultural property === [[File:北杜市明野町上手 宇波刀神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|300px|Torii of Uwato Shrine]] * Stone torii of Uwato Shrine (Yamanashi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property, designated April 8, 1971)[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai_data/yamanashinobunkazai_kenzoubutsu009.html Yamanashi cultural property guide (database) Architecture 09] Yamanashi Prefecture === Access === *About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sudama Interchange|Q11664609|WD=Q11664609|lt=Sudama IC|1=Sutama interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Expressway|Q1091276|WD=Q1091276|1=Chūō Expressway}}.
